title: eugenia alpestris
intro: Eugenia alpestris, also known as the mountain white guava, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, making it a valuable medicinal plant. Eugenia alpestris has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
